Fix histogram metrics with programmatic MeterFilter instead of YAML property

meter names. Use a MeterFilter bean to reliably enable histogram buckets
for http.server.requests.

Rebuild coprocessor as direct injection (read/write hooks) replacing virtual tokens

Virtual token approach failed — attention bottleneck killed gradient (copro_hebb_rule=0.0001).
New design: read hook captures hidden state at layer 9, coprocessor processes through
3 DifferentiablePatch layers, write hook injects delta at layer 27. No attention needed.

commit.message:
perf: serve Vosk model via vosk-model:// custom protocol — no 40MB IPC transfer

Previously VoskWakeEngine called getWakeModelData() which read the 40MB zip,
serialized it as an ArrayBuffer over Electron IPC, then re-created a blob URL
in the renderer every boot. This caused a 5-8s startup delay.

New flow:
- Main: register vosk-model:// protocol (registerBufferProtocol) that serves
  the cached zip directly from userData/vosk-models/
- Main: voice:wake:ensure-model IPC — download+validate only, no bytes returned
- Preload: expose ensureWakeModel() alongside legacy getWakeModelData()
- Renderer: call ensureWakeModel(), then createModel('vosk-model://model.zip')
- CSP: add vosk-model: to default-src and connect-src

Result: after first download, model loads via direct protocol fetch instead
of a 40MB IPC round-trip. Startup time drops ~4-6 seconds.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Parallelize judge and explain calls in Pipeline B

Step 3 (judge): previously sequential for-loop over all drafts, now uses
asyncio.gather for the first-pass judge calls in parallel. Revisions for
below-A drafts remain sequential (usually 0-1 drafts). This cuts the judge
step from N×45s to max(45s) regardless of draft count.

Step 5 (explain): sequential loop replaced with asyncio.gather, so M
explanation calls now cost max(45s) instead of M×45s.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ci: restrict CI workflow to main branch pushes only

The bare `on: push` trigger was causing CI to run on tag pushes too,
duplicating the work already done by the release workflow.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Integrate officer intelligence into letter gen + evidence push notifications

1. Officer history in letter generation: When generating a contest letter,
   looks up the issuing officer's badge from detected_tickets, queries
   hearing_officer_patterns and officer_win_rates (FOIA fallback) for
   dismissal rates. Injects officer tendency (lenient/strict/neutral)
   and strategy guidance into the Claude prompt so the letter is
   calibrated to the specific officer's track record.

2. Evidence gathering push notification: After detecting a new ticket
   and sending the evidence request email, sends a push notification
   via FCM with the violation type, amount, days remaining, and the
   top evidence question from the violation-specific guidance. Non-
   critical — failure doesn't block the email flow.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

feat(pr-review): fetch upstream release notes for Dependabot PRs (#96)

For Dependabot PRs, the script now fetches release notes directly from
the upstream GitHub repository instead of relying on the Dependabot PR
body, which often contains only boilerplate.

- Parse package name and version range from PR title
- Strip registry prefixes (ghcr.io, docker.io, quay.io) to resolve the
  GitHub owner/repo
- Fetch GitHub releases between old and new version, skipping
  alpha/rc/testing tags that break semver parsing
- Fall back to Dependabot PR body when no GitHub releases are found
- Add linked release tags (e.g. v2.0.29 | v2.0.30) to the PR comment
  for quick navigation to the upstream changelog

Co-authored-by: Stefan <githubspiev@gmail.com>

commit.message:
docs: add file sandbox system design

Core features:
- file_read: read files within whitelist directories
- file_write: create new files or append to existing files only
- file_edit: precise edit with exact text matching
- file_delete: move files to trash (timestamp-based isolation)

Security:
- Path whitelist validation
- Forbidden extensions
- Max file size limit
- Trash-based deletion (recoverable)

Trash system:
- Each delete creates a timestamp directory (YYYYMMDDHHmmssfff)
- Prevents naming conflicts between deletions
- Managed via API, not exposed as LLM tools

Fix runner + add HTTP/HTTPS stream support

runner 오류 수정:
- Xcode: /Applications/Xcode_16.2.app → /Applications/Xcode.app (심볼릭 링크)
- sudo gem install cocoapods 제거 (macos-15 권한 충돌)
- pod install --repo-update (사전 설치된 CocoaPods 사용)

HTTP/HTTPS 스트림 지원:
- Podfile: ffmpeg-kit-ios → ffmpeg-kit-ios-https (HTTPS 필요)
- RTSPDecoder: rtsp:// 와 http/https 프로토콜 옵션 분기 처리
  · RTSP: rtsp_transport=tcp, stimeout
  · HTTP/HTTPS: timeout, reconnect, reconnect_streamed, multiple_requests
- ViewController: rtsp/rtsps/http/https URL 모두 허용
- UI: placeholder 및 에러 메시지 업데이트

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: move playwright/stitch to user-level MCP (claude mcp add -s user)

Plugin .mcp.json caching is unreliable for npx-based MCP servers.
User-level MCP via `claude mcp add -s user` is more robust:
- Writes directly to ~/.claude.json, always loaded
- Not affected by plugin cache staleness
- Simpler install: one command, no plugin rebuild needed

Changes:
- Remove playwright and stitch from plugin .mcp.json
- Update setup.md: simplified install flow using claude mcp add
- Update skill-commons.md: new install commands in registry

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(core): unify URI resolution via template, remove fragment/document branching

Replace isFragmentMode() with template-driven buildSuffixFromTemplate().
Generalize extractIdFromTemplate → extractVarsFromTemplate for all named variables.
Simplify document-resolver resolve methods to a single linear flow.

feat: 3x desktop font sizes — html base 48px, nav/h1 in explicit px

Sets html { font-size: 48px } (3× the 16px browser default) in every
page's @media (min-width: 1024px) block, making all rem-based content
text 3× its prior size on desktop. Nav logo/links and page H1 are
re-expressed in absolute px values so they stay at controlled sizes
and don't inherit the 48px scaling.

commit.message:
v2.0.0: Multi-pass video analysis pipeline

Replace single-pass Gemini+Claude pipeline with 5-phase analysis:
- Phase 1: Structure scan (Gemini LOW, full video → gameplay segments)
- Phase 2: Segment deep analysis (Gemini MEDIUM, clipped per segment)
- Phase 3: Player-focused passes (Gemini MEDIUM, per team)
- Phase 4: Comparative rating (Claude, rank-then-rate with evidence)
- Phase 5: Calibration challenge (Claude, devil's advocate ±0.5 adjustment)

New endpoints: analyze-structure, analyze-segments, analyze-player-focus,
analyze-rate, analyze-calibrate. Client orchestrates sequentially with
granular phase progress. Falls back to single-pass if no Gemini file URI.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(mobile): add raw-format WebView prover for trustless transfers

ZkProverProvider now exposes both byte-array and raw snarkjs proof
formats via the WebView. generateRawProofViaWebView returns the
proof in string-array format (pi_a/pi_b/pi_c as field element
strings) for relayer compatibility, while keeping spending_key
local to the device.
